1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: Who spoke to Joshua after the death of Moses?
questions.explanation: Joshua 1:1 says the LORD spoke unto Joshua after Moses died.

questions.question: What command did God give Joshua in Joshua 1:2?

options.B: Arise and go over this Jordan

questions.explanation: The LORD told Joshua to arise and go over the Jordan with the people.

questions.question: What land promise did God make to Joshua in chapter 1?

options.A: Every place the sole of your foot shall tread upon

questions.explanation: God promised all the land the feet of Joshua and the people would tread upon.

questions.question: Which river marks the eastern boundary of the promised land in Joshua 1:4?
questions.explanation: Joshua 1:4 references the river Euphrates as the eastern limit.

questions.question: How long did God say no one would be able to stand before Joshua?

options.A: All the days of thy life

questions.explanation: God said no man would be able to stand before Joshua all the days of his life.

questions.question: What two qualities did God command Joshua to have?
questions.explanation: God commanded Joshua to be strong and courageous.

questions.question: According to Joshua 1:7, what should Joshua not turn from?
questions.explanation: He was told not to turn from the law that Moses commanded him.

questions.question: What is Joshua told to do with the book of the law?

options.D: Meditate on it day and night

questions.explanation: Joshua 1:8 commands meditation on the law day and night.

questions.question: What reason is given for not being afraid?

options.A: The LORD thy God is with thee

questions.explanation: God reassured Joshua that He would be with him wherever he went.

questions.question: How many days did Joshua tell the people to prepare to pass over Jordan?
questions.explanation: Joshua commanded them to prepare victuals for three days before crossing.

questions.question: Which tribes were told to remain on this side of Jordan with their children and cattle?

options.A: Reubenites, Gadites, and half the tribe of Manasseh

questions.explanation: Those tribes were instructed to stay until their brethren had rest.

questions.question: What did the people promise Joshua in Joshua 1:16?

options.C: We will go back to Egypt
options.D: We will do all that you command us

questions.explanation: They promised to obey Joshua as they obeyed Moses.

questions.question: Whom did the people want to be with Joshua as they had been with Moses?
questions.explanation: They said only that the LORD be with Joshua as He was with Moses.

questions.question: What would happen to anyone who rebelled against Joshua's commandment?

options.C: They would be put in prison
options.D: They would be put to death

questions.explanation: Joshua said rebels would be put to death.

questions.question: Who did Joshua send to spy out Jericho?
questions.explanation: Joshua sent two men to secretly spy out Jericho.

questions.question: Where did the two spies lodge in Jericho?
questions.explanation: They stayed in the house of Rahab the harlot.

questions.question: What did the king of Jericho ask Rahab to do?

options.A: Bring forth the men who came to her house

questions.explanation: The king demanded the two men be brought out.

questions.question: What did Rahab say when the king’s men came to her house?

options.A: They are in the king's palace
options.B: I know not whence they are

questions.explanation: She claimed she did not know where they came from.

questions.question: How did Rahab hide the men?

options.A: With the stalks of flax on the roof

questions.explanation: She hid them under flax stalks on the roof.

questions.question: Why did Rahab say the people fainted?

options.B: Because the LORD had given the land into Israel's hand

questions.explanation: She said their terror fell on the inhabitants because the LORD gave the land to Israel.

questions.question: What mighty act did Rahab mention Israel had done?

options.C: Dried up the Red Sea water
options.D: Defeated the Amorites in one battle

questions.explanation: She referred to God drying up the Red Sea for Israel.

questions.question: What did Rahab say the people of the land knew about Israel’s God?

options.B: He is God in heaven above and earth beneath
options.C: He lives only in the wilderness

questions.explanation: She acknowledged the LORD is God in heaven above and earth beneath.

questions.question: What did Rahab ask the spies to do for her family?

options.A: Deal kindly with my father's household
options.B: Give us a house in Israel

questions.explanation: She requested kindness for her family because she showed them kindness.

questions.question: How did Rahab help the spies escape?

options.B: By letting them out through the gate
options.D: By letting them down through a window with a cord

questions.explanation: She lowered them through a window with a cord.

questions.question: What would happen if Rahab told the king about the spies?

options.C: The men would be quit of the oath

questions.explanation: They said if she betrayed them they would be free from her oath.

questions.question: What was Rahab instructed to bind in her window?
questions.explanation: She was told to bind a scarlet thread in the window.

questions.question: What would happen if anyone left Rahab's house?

options.B: Their blood would be on their own head

questions.explanation: The spies said anyone who leaves the house bears their own blood.

questions.question: What did the spies report to Joshua after returning?

options.A: The LORD hath delivered into our hands all the land

questions.explanation: They reported that the land had been delivered into their hands.

questions.question: Where did Israel camp before crossing Jordan?
questions.explanation: They lodged by Jordan before passing over.

questions.question: What were the people told to do when they saw the ark?

options.B: Remove from your place and go after it

questions.explanation: They were told to follow the ark.

questions.question: How far were the people to stay from the ark?
questions.explanation: They were told to keep about two thousand cubits away.

questions.question: What were the people told to do because the LORD would do wonders?
questions.explanation: Joshua told the people to sanctify themselves.

questions.question: Who bore the ark of the covenant into Jordan?
questions.explanation: The priests carrying the ark went before the people.

questions.question: What would happen when the priests' feet touched the water?

options.B: The waters would be cut off and stand in a heap

questions.explanation: The waters of Jordan stood in a heap when the priests carried the ark into the water.

questions.question: How did the Israelites cross Jordan?
questions.explanation: They passed over Jordan on dry ground.

questions.question: What did the LORD command after all the people had passed over Jordan?

options.A: Take twelve men and take stones out of Jordan

questions.explanation: God commanded them to take twelve stones from the river.

questions.question: How many stones was each man to carry?
questions.explanation: Each of the twelve men was to take up one stone upon his shoulder.

questions.question: Where did Joshua set up the twelve stones taken from Jordan?

options.A: At the altar of the LORD
options.B: In the midst of Jordan at the place where the priests stood

questions.explanation: The stones were set up in the midst of Jordan where the priests’ feet stood.

questions.question: What were the stones a sign of?

options.C: The crossing of Jordan on dry ground

questions.explanation: They served as a memorial of the crossing of Jordan.

questions.question: What did Joshua say the stones would remind their children of?

options.B: That Israel came over Jordan on dry land

questions.explanation: He explained they would remind future generations of the dry crossing.

questions.question: Why did the LORD do this miracle at Jordan?

options.A: To show off to the nations
options.D: So all the earth would know the hand of the LORD and fear Him forever

questions.explanation: The miracle showed the LORD's mighty power so people would fear Him.

questions.question: What did the LORD tell Joshua to make at Gilgal?

options.A: Sharp knives and circumcise the Israelites again

questions.explanation: God commanded a second circumcision of Israel.

questions.question: Where did Joshua circumcise the people?
questions.explanation: Joshua circumcised the Israelites at the hill of the foreskins.

questions.question: What did the LORD say had been rolled away from Israel?
questions.explanation: The LORD said the reproach of Egypt was rolled away.

questions.question: What festival did Israel keep at Gilgal?
questions.explanation: They kept the Passover on the fourteenth day at Gilgal.

questions.question: What ceased after the Israelites ate the old corn of the land?
questions.explanation: The manna ceased when they ate the produce of Canaan.

questions.question: Who appeared to Joshua near Jericho with his sword drawn?
questions.explanation: A man stood before Joshua with a sword drawn.

questions.question: What title did the man with the sword give himself?

options.A: Captain of the host of the LORD

questions.explanation: He said he was the captain of the Lord's host.

questions.question: What did Joshua do when the man spoke to him?

options.C: Fell on his face and worshipped

questions.explanation: Joshua fell on his face and worshipped the man.

questions.question: What city was shut up because of the children of Israel?
questions.explanation: Jericho was tightly shut because of Israel.

questions.question: What had the LORD already given Joshua in Joshua 6:2?
questions.explanation: God told Joshua He had given Jericho and its king into his hand.

questions.question: How many days were the people to compass Jericho?
questions.explanation: They were commanded to march around the city once for six days.

questions.question: What did the priests carry before the ark?
questions.explanation: Seven priests bore seven trumpets before the ark.

questions.question: What would happen when the people shouted after the long blast?

options.A: The wall of the city would fall flat

questions.explanation: The wall would fall flat upon the shout.

questions.question: What were the people told not to do until the commanded day?
questions.explanation: They were told to remain silent until Joshua said shout.

questions.question: What happened on the seventh day of marching around Jericho?

options.D: They compassed the city seven times

questions.explanation: On the seventh day they marched around Jericho seven times.

questions.question: Who was to be spared from the accursed thing in Jericho?
questions.explanation: Rahab and all that were with her were to live.

questions.question: What would make the camp of Israel a curse?
questions.explanation: Taking accursed things would make the camp accursed.

questions.question: What was the sign that the wall had fallen?

options.A: The people shouted with a great shout

questions.explanation: The shout following the trumpets signaled the wall's fall.

questions.question: What was done to the inhabitants of Jericho?

options.A: Utterly destroyed them with the edge of the sword

questions.explanation: All that was in Jericho was utterly destroyed.

questions.question: What did Joshua command the spies to do after Jericho fell?

options.B: Bring out Rahab and her family

questions.explanation: Joshua sent for Rahab and her household as promised.

questions.question: What became of Rahab and her household?

options.C: They were brought outside the camp and left alive

questions.explanation: Rahab and her family were saved and left outside the camp.

questions.question: Why did Rahab dwell in Israel?

options.C: Because she renounced her old trade

questions.explanation: She was spared for hiding the spies.

questions.question: What curse did Joshua pronounce on Jericho?

options.A: The city will never be rebuilt
options.B: Anyone rebuilding it will lose his firstborn and youngest son
options.D: The site will be forever an altar

questions.explanation: He cursed anyone who rebuilt Jericho so they would lose their firstborn and youngest son.

questions.question: What caused the LORD's anger against Israel after Jericho?

options.A: Achan took of the accursed thing
options.B: They did not circumcise the people
options.C: They refused to march against Ai

questions.explanation: Achan's sin with the accursed things brought God’s anger.

questions.question: How many men did Joshua initially take to fight Ai?
questions.explanation: Joshua sent about three thousand men to Ai.

questions.question: How many men of Israel were killed at Ai?
questions.explanation: Thirty-six Israelite men were struck down at Ai.

questions.question: What did Joshua do when he heard of the defeat at Ai?

options.A: Rent his clothes and fell on his face before the ark

questions.explanation: He tore his clothes and worshipped before the ark.

questions.question: Why could Israel not stand before their enemies?

options.B: Because the city was too strong
options.C: Because they took of the accursed thing

questions.explanation: The sin of taking accursed things made them accursed.

questions.question: What did the LORD command Israel to do before the next day?

options.D: Sanctify themselves and find the accursed thing

questions.explanation: They were told to sanctify themselves and remove the accursed thing.

questions.question: Which tribe was taken when the accursed thing was identified?
questions.explanation: The tribe of Judah was selected, and within it the family of the Zerahites was taken.

questions.question: What did Achan confess to taking from Jericho?

options.D: A Babylonish garment, silver, and a wedge of gold

questions.explanation: Achan confessed to coveting and taking those specific items.

questions.question: Why did Achan take the accursed items?

options.C: Because he wanted to trade them
options.D: Because he thought they were innocent

questions.explanation: He admitted he coveted the items and hid them.

questions.question: What was done to Achan and his possession?

options.B: He and all that he had were stoned and burned

questions.explanation: Achan and his belongings were destroyed by stoning and fire.

questions.question: What did the LORD tell Joshua about Ai after the sin was removed?

options.A: Fear not; I have given it into thy hand
options.B: It is too strong to take

questions.explanation: God assured Joshua that Ai would be given into his hand.

questions.question: How many men did Joshua choose to lie in ambush against Ai?
questions.explanation: Joshua selected thirty thousand mighty men of valor.

questions.question: What did Joshua tell his ambush to do after the main force fled?

options.B: Rise up quickly and seize the city

questions.explanation: They were to rise quickly and take the city when drawn out.

questions.question: What was the purpose of the ambush at Ai?

options.D: To seize the city when it was overextended

questions.explanation: The ambush was meant to capture Ai after drawing out its forces.

questions.question: How many times did Joshua and the people surround Ai before the city fell?

options.C: Once after the ambush was revealed

questions.explanation: After the ambush was triggered, they surrounded Ai and took it.

questions.question: What did the Israelites do to the men of Ai?

options.A: Slew them with the edge of the sword

questions.explanation: They killed the men of Ai in battle.

questions.question: How did Joshua execute the king of Ai?

options.C: He sent him back to Jericho
options.D: He brought him to Joshua and then killed him

questions.explanation: The king was brought to Joshua and then executed.

questions.question: What did Joshua do to the city of Ai after taking it?

options.B: Burned it and made it a heap forever
options.C: Turned it into a refuge city

questions.explanation: Joshua burned the city and made it an everlasting heap.

questions.question: Which mountain did Joshua build an altar on?
questions.explanation: Joshua built an altar to the LORD on Mount Ebal.

questions.question: What type of stones were used for the altar on Mount Ebal?

options.A: Stones with iron made on them
options.C: Whole stones over which no iron was lifted

questions.explanation: The altar was made of whole stones over which no iron instrument was lifted.

questions.question: What did Joshua write on the stones of the altar?

options.B: A copy of the law of Moses

questions.explanation: He wrote a copy of the law on the stones.

questions.question: Who listened when Joshua read all the words of the law?

options.A: All Israel with women, little ones, and strangers

questions.explanation: Joshua read the law before all the congregation including women and strangers.

questions.question: What reaction did the kings of the land have when they heard about Israel’s conquests?

options.C: They gathered to fight with Joshua

questions.explanation: They united to fight Joshua and Israel.

questions.question: What did the people of Gibeon appear to be in Joshua 9:3?
questions.explanation: They came pretending to be ambassadors from a distant land.

questions.question: What disguise did the Gibeonites use to appear as travelers?

options.A: Old sacks, rent wine bottles, old shoes, and moldy bread

questions.explanation: They made themselves look like people from a long journey.

questions.question: Why did Israel make a treaty with the Gibeonites?

options.A: They asked not counsel at the mouth of the LORD

questions.explanation: The Israelites made the covenant without consulting God.

questions.question: What discovery was made three days after the treaty with Gibeon?

options.C: They were neighbors living near them

questions.explanation: Israel discovered the Gibeonites lived nearby, not far away.

questions.question: What did the princes decide regarding the Gibeonites?

options.B: Let them live as woodcutters and water carriers

questions.explanation: They allowed them to live but as servants for the congregation.

questions.question: How did Joshua describe the Gibeonites' deception?
questions.explanation: Joshua charged them with deceiving Israel by pretending to be from afar.

questions.question: Which king feared greatly when he heard about Israel’s victories?
questions.explanation: Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem was greatly afraid.

questions.question: Who were the five kings that joined against Gibeon?

options.A: Kings of Ai, Jericho, Hebron, Gaza, and Lachish
options.B: Kings of Jerusalem, Hebron, Jarmuth, Lachish, and Eglon
options.C: Kings of Samaria, Tyre, Sidon, Lebanon, and Hazor
options.D: Kings of Moab, Ammon, Edom, Philistia, and Syria

questions.explanation: Those five kings came to fight Gibeon.

questions.question: What did the LORD say to Joshua about the battle at Gibeon?

options.A: Fear them not; I have delivered them into thy hand

questions.explanation: God told Joshua not to fear and that He would deliver the enemy.

questions.question: What did Joshua do when the LORD discomfited the Amorites?

options.C: He came suddenly and attacked them at night

questions.explanation: Joshua rose quickly and attacked the Amorites while they were discomfited.

questions.question: What extraordinary event struck the fleeing Amorites?
questions.explanation: The LORD cast down great stones from heaven on them.

questions.question: What did Joshua command the sun and moon to do?
questions.explanation: Joshua asked the sun and moon to stand still until Israel avenged themselves.

questions.question: What was the result of Joshua’s prayer for the sun and moon?

options.B: The sun stood still for about a whole day

questions.explanation: The sun stood still and delayed going down about a whole day.

questions.question: Why is the day when the sun stood still described as unique?

options.A: Because it was the day of the Passover
options.B: Because it rained in the land of Canaan
options.C: Because the walls of Jericho fell
options.D: Because God never before or after hearkened to a man like that

questions.explanation: The verse emphasizes the uniqueness of God's response to Joshua's prayer.

questions.question: What strategic advice did God give Joshua regarding pursuit after the battle?

options.B: Suffer them not to enter their cities

questions.explanation: God told Joshua to pursue the enemies and not allow them to escape to their cities.

questions.question: Why were the five kings brought out of the cave before Israel?

options.A: To show the people their victory and demonstrate God’s deliverance

questions.explanation: Joshua displayed the captured kings to the people after victory.

questions.question: Why did Joshua command the captains to put their feet on the necks of the kings?

options.D: To show they were under Israel's authority

questions.explanation: It symbolized their submission and Israel's victory.

questions.question: What does the curse upon the man who rebuilds Jericho reveal about Israel's relationship to the city?

options.A: Jericho was accursed and reserved for the LORD
options.B: The site was meant for a future temple
options.C: The city belonged to their enemies

questions.explanation: The curse emphasizes Jericho's sacred status after being devoted to destruction.

questions.question: What instructions did the LORD give Joshua before the battle by the waters of Merom?

options.B: Be not afraid; tomorrow I will deliver them slain into thy hand

questions.explanation: God reassured Joshua that He would deliver their enemies into Israel's hand.

questions.question: What does the sudden attack at Merom illustrate about Joshua’s leadership?

options.C: His willingness to obey God and seize opportunity

questions.explanation: The surprise attack showed Joshua’s obedience and bold action.

questions.question: What was distinctive about Hazor compared to other cities Joshua captured?

options.A: It was the head of all those kingdoms
options.B: It was a city of refuge

questions.explanation: Hazor was the chief city of the confederation and was burned.

questions.question: Why did Israel burn Hazor but not other fortified cities?

options.A: Because it was full of idols
options.C: Because it was the chief city and represented the enemy’s strength
options.D: Because it was commanded by Moses

questions.explanation: Hazor was burned as the principal city of the alliance.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'Joshua made war a long time with all those kings' imply about the conquest?

options.D: The campaign was extensive and required persistent effort

questions.explanation: The conquest involved prolonged campaigns against many kings.

questions.question: Why did God harden the hearts of the enemy kings?

options.B: So He might destroy them utterly and prevent favor toward them

questions.explanation: God hardened their hearts so Israel could carry out His judgment.

questions.question: What does the removal of the Anakims from the land signify for Israel’s conquest?

options.A: The elimination of the most terrifying foes
options.D: The end of the line of Judah

questions.explanation: Destroying the Anakims removed the feared giants from the land.

questions.question: What is the significance of the final verse of chapter 11?

options.C: Joshua completed the conquest as the LORD had promised Moses

questions.explanation: It declares Joshua took all the land according to God's promise.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the list of kings in Joshua 12?

options.B: To record the kings defeated by Moses and Joshua
options.D: To list the tribes of Israel

questions.explanation: Joshua 12 catalogs the kings conquered during the conquest.

questions.question: Why are Sihon and Og included among the defeated kings?

options.A: They were defeated before crossing Jordan and were part of the conquest

questions.explanation: Moses and Israel defeated them east of Jordan before the land division.

questions.question: What does the mention of Og being a remnant of giants indicate?

options.D: He belonged to the feared Rephaim dynasty

questions.explanation: Og is described as a remaining giant leader among a feared people.

questions.question: What does God's statement to Joshua about his age imply?

options.A: There remained much land yet to possess

questions.explanation: God told Joshua he was old and much of the land still remained to be taken.

questions.question: What region east of Jordan is listed as remaining land?

options.B: All the borders of the Philistines and Geshuri

questions.explanation: The remaining land included the Philistine border and Geshur.

questions.question: Why was the land of the Giblites and Lebanon included in the remaining conquest?

options.C: It had not yet been fully driven out
options.D: It was given to the Levites

questions.explanation: These regions were still inhabited and were yet to be conquered.

questions.question: Why did the tribe of Levi receive no inheritance of land?

options.A: Their inheritance was the LORD Himself and the offerings made by fire
options.C: They had already received cities east of Jordan

questions.explanation: Levi's inheritance was their priestly service and the LORD.

questions.question: What does Caleb’s request reveal about his character?

options.D: He wanted more land than others

questions.explanation: Caleb requested the mountain region because he was still strong and faithful.

questions.question: What choice does Joshua present to the people?

options.B: Choose whom you will serve, the LORD or foreign gods

questions.explanation: He challenged them to decide between serving the LORD or other gods.

questions.question: How did the people answer Joshua’s challenge?
questions.explanation: They declared they would serve the LORD.

questions.question: Why did Joshua warn the people not to rebel against the LORD?

options.B: Because foreign nations would envy them
options.C: Because the LORD is holy and will not pardon transgression
options.D: Because they would lose their land

questions.explanation: Joshua emphasized God's holiness and intolerance of rebellion.

questions.question: What covenant action did Joshua perform after the people agreed to serve the LORD?

options.A: He wrote these words in the book of the law and took a great stone
options.C: He built a new altar of gold
options.D: He set up a monument of bronze

questions.explanation: He recorded their words and set up a stone as a witness.

questions.question: What purpose did the great stone at Shechem serve?

options.D: A witness to the covenant between Israel and the LORD

questions.explanation: It served as a witness to Israel’s covenant commitment.

questions.question: What does the final record of Joshua’s death signify about his life?

options.A: He served the LORD faithfully all his days

questions.explanation: The conclusion states Joshua served the LORD all his days.

questions.question: Why should the nations know the hand of the LORD and fear Him forever?

options.B: So they would recognize God's power and remain in awe

questions.explanation: The miraculous crossing would cause all peoples to fear the LORD.

questions.question: What does Rahab’s declaration about God reveal about the reputation of Israel’s God?

options.A: He is universally feared and known as God in heaven above and earth beneath
options.B: He is only a local god
options.C: He is weak before the Canaanites

questions.explanation: Her statement shows God’s supremacy was recognized among the nations.

questions.question: What is the promised result of meditating on the law day and night?
questions.explanation: Meditation on the law is linked to prosperity and success.

questions.question: What was the significance of celebrating Passover at Gilgal?

options.A: It marked Israel’s first observance of Passover in the promised land
options.B: It replaced the Passover in Egypt
options.C: It was the last Passover ever observed
options.D: It was only for the priests

questions.explanation: They kept Passover after crossing into Canaan, signifying a new chapter.

questions.question: Why were silver, gold, brass, and iron consecrated unto the LORD?

options.D: Because they were accursed things dedicated to the LORD

questions.explanation: The metals were set apart as holy treasure of the Lord.

questions.question: What does the requirement to sanctify the people before removing the accursed thing suggest?

options.B: Holiness must precede obedience and deliverance
options.C: The people were to fast for seven days

questions.explanation: Sanctification prepared Israel to act in obedience and purity.

questions.question: Why was an altar built on Mount Ebal as Moses commanded?

options.A: To renew covenant obedience and proclaim blessings and cursings

questions.explanation: The altar was part of covenant renewal in the land.

questions.question: What lesson arises from Israel’s failure to consult the LORD about the Gibeonites?

options.A: It is acceptable to ignore God in minor matters
options.C: Consulting God is essential before making treaties
options.D: Treaties with neighbors are always wrong

questions.explanation: The event shows the importance of seeking God’s guidance.

questions.question: What does the sun standing still demonstrate about the relationship between prayer and God’s actions?

options.A: God may act miraculously in response to earnest prayer
options.D: The sun always stands still during battles

questions.explanation: It illustrates God’s power to intervene when asked.

questions.question: How did divine intervention influence Joshua’s military strategy at Merom?

options.B: He attacked suddenly because God promised victory

questions.explanation: God’s promise of victory enabled a sudden and confident attack.

questions.question: What is the significance of burning Hazor in the conquest narrative?

options.A: It was the only city with a temple
options.B: It was spared by the LORD
options.D: It symbolized the destruction of the leadership of the enemy confederation

questions.explanation: Hazor’s destruction showed the defeat of the enemy coalition’s head city.

questions.question: What call to action does Joshua make regarding idols?

options.C: Put away the gods which your fathers served

questions.explanation: Joshua urged Israel to abandon the idols of their ancestors.

questions.question: What is the consequence Joshua warns if the people serve foreign gods?

options.A: The LORD will not pardon them
options.C: They will be welcomed by their neighbors

questions.explanation: Joshua made clear that disobedience would not be pardoned by God.

questions.question: Why was writing the covenant in the book of the law important?

options.A: To make a legal document for foreigners
options.B: To preserve the commitment and hold the people accountable
options.C: To hide it from the kings

questions.explanation: Recording the covenant ensured the people could remember and be accountable.

questions.question: How does God’s promise to Joshua compare to His promise to Moses?

options.B: It is equally sure: I will not fail thee nor forsake thee
options.D: It is only for a short time

questions.explanation: The promise mirrors God’s commitment to Moses: He will not fail or forsake Joshua.

questions.question: Why was sanctification necessary before the crossing of Jordan?

options.C: To show respect for the river
options.D: To set the people apart for a divine miracle

questions.explanation: Sanctification prepared them to witness God’s miraculous action.

questions.question: What theological principle is shown by Rahab’s preservation?

options.C: Faithful action and mercy can protect the believer

questions.explanation: Rahab’s faith and kindness led to her and her family’s preservation.

questions.question: What does Achan’s punishment teach about communal responsibility?

options.A: Individual sins are ignored by the community
options.B: One person's sin can bring judgment on the whole community

questions.explanation: The community suffered until the hidden sin was removed.

questions.question: What is the purpose of reading the law after the conquest of Ai?

options.A: To remind Israel of God's requirements after victory
options.B: To reenact the giving of the law

questions.explanation: Reading the law renewed Israel’s commitment after success in battle.

questions.question: Why did the princes not break the treaty with Gibeon despite the deception?

options.C: They feared wrath because of the oath sworn by the LORD

questions.explanation: They kept the oath because it had been sworn before the LORD.

questions.question: What does the prayer for the sun and moon tell us about Israel’s view of creation?

options.A: They worshiped the sun and moon
options.C: They thought the sun was a god

questions.explanation: The request acknowledges God's sovereign control over the heavenly bodies.

questions.question: How does Joshua contrast the LORD with the gods of the nations?

options.A: He commands Israel to fear the LORD and put away foreign gods
options.B: He says the LORD is one of many gods
options.C: He allows them to serve both
options.D: He says foreign gods are better

questions.explanation: Joshua exhorts Israel to serve the LORD alone and abandon foreign worship.

questions.question: What does the burial of Eleazar in a hill given to Phinehas indicate about priestly inheritance?

options.C: Priests received their own allotted inheritance within Israel
options.D: Priests were buried in enemy territory

questions.explanation: Eleazar’s burial on land given to Phinehas confirms the Levites and priests received designated land.
